sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund the Cardano Builder DAO as a smart contract-governed, KPI-driven funding mechanism for Cardano builders.
Recipient: Cardano Builder DAO, with treasury withdrawal received and administered by an independent council composed of dRep DAO members before milestone-based disbursement.

Deliverables: Allocate capital to builders expected to advance Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs., Require funded projects to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions., Coordinate proposal evaluation, feedback, transparent fund distribution, and results tracking., Operate milestone-based disbursement from an independent multisig after administrator validation., Return unused or unallocated DAO treasury funds to the Cardano Treasury.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The Cardano Builder DAO is proposed as an ecosystem-wide, smart contract-governed funding mechanism for projects advancing Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The DAO intends to allocate capital to builders who can improve monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal states that funded projects are required to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The DAO says it built Version 1 and Version 2 of a KPI dashboard, moving from self-reported KPI tracking toward live on-chain Cardano data.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The CB DAO claims it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Round 1 is described as involving 38 voting members, 27 requesting members, 20 funded companies, 83% governance participation, and 5,541,335 ADA distributed through a smart contract-governed process.
missing: Independent evidence for: The DAO intends to allocate capital to builders who can improve monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL.
Independent evidence for: The DAO says it built Version 1 and Version 2 of a KPI dashboard, moving from self-reported KPI tracking toward live on-chain Cardano data.
Independent evidence for: The CB DAO claims it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.

Independent evidence for: Round 1 is described as involving 38 voting members, 27 requesting members, 20 funded companies, 83% governance participation, and 5,541,335 ADA distributed through a smart contract-governed process.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The Cardano Builder DAO is proposed as an ecosystem-wide, smart contract-governed funding mechanism for projects advancing Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The DAO intends to allocate capital to builders who can improve monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
